title: build: accept branch and subdirectory when context is `git` repo
body:
When using `build -t <tag> <some-url-to-git-repo>` buildah does not
accepts syntax `url#branch:subdir` following commit allows end users to
switch context to any subdirectory in the git repo.

Usage
```console
buildah build -t test 'https://github.com/containers/podman.git#main:contrib/hello'
```

Closes: https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/3989

Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [flouthoc] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

> Current version appears to only accept subdirectory names if a branch name is provided, while I'm not aware that specifying either of them should require specifying the other. Needs to update the documentation.

@nalind Docker supports `myrepo.git#somebranch:subdir` or `myrepo.git#:somedir` for default branch added a test to verify for both of them.
